ogTitle: Cortlinye vs Corunna Station: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cortlinye and Corunna Station.
heading: Cortlinye vs Corunna Station — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cortlinye, South Australia and Corunna Station, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cortlinye has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Corunna Station (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cortlinye or Corunna Station safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cortlinye has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Corunna Station scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cortlinye scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Corunna Station scores 17/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cortlinye or Corunna Station?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cortlinye has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
